**When to Use Sub-Agents**

* Only when a task naturally decomposes into many independent research threads that benefit from parallelism — e.g., the user asks multiple unrelated questions, or a single request requires analyzing many separate areas of a codebase independently, especially if the codebase is large.

* For complex cross-cutting investigations — tracing flows across many modules in a large or unfamiliar codebase — explore can be faster.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me analyze the issues:

**Root-level files:**

1. **`Directory.Build.targets` line 3**: `<Import Project="$(RepoRoot)eng\custom-analyzers.props" />` — This is an unconditional import. If the file doesn't exist (fresh clone, or it's generated), the build breaks. Should use `Condition="Exists(...)"`.

2. **`Directory.Build.targets` lines 6-8**: `<CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets>` is set in Directory.Build.targets. This property is read by MSBuild *before* common targets are imported. 

But Directory.Build.targets is imported *after* Microsoft.Common.targets, so by the time this property is set, MSBuild has already processed the CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets hook. This is why the NuGet pre-build hook stopped working — it needs to be in Directory.Build.props instead.

3. **`Directory.Build.targets` lines 11-13**: The `RunCodeGen` target lacks extensibility points. Other teams can't hook into pre or post processing, so it should define empty `BeforeRunCodeGen`/`AfterRunCodeGen` targets or use `DependsOnTargets` to allow customization.

4. **`hard/Directory.Build.props` lines 9-10**: There's a circular dependency here — the condition checks if `CommonPropsImported` is true before importing the file that defines it, so the import never actually happens.

5. **`hard/Directory.Build.props` lines 3-6**: The wildcard import flags for NuGet package `.props` files are disabled, which prevents automatic loading of build artifacts from package folders.

6. **`hard/Directory.Build.targets` line 4**: Same timing issue as the root — custom targets are set too late.

7. **`hard/Directory.Build.targets` line 8**: The code-analysis.targets import depends on `RepoToolsPath` from the never-imported Common.props, and there's no existence check to handle the failure gracefully.

8. **`hard/Directory.Build.targets` line 11**: Looking at the GenerateAssemblyInfo target...

The target name conflicts with the SDK's built-in one, and the RunPostBuildChecks target depends on an internal `_CopyFilesMarkedCopyLocal` target that shouldn't be relied on. Now I need to address all these problems systematically.

<Project>
  <!-- Import shared analyzer configuration only when the file exists -->
  <Import Project="$(RepoRoot)eng\custom-analyzers.props"
      Condition="Exists('$(RepoRoot)eng\custom-analyzers.props')" />

  <!--

    the CustomBefore/After hooks. The property was moved to Directory.Build.props.
  -->

  <!-- Extensible code-generation pipeline: teams can override
       RunCodeGenDependsOn in their own .targets to inject steps. -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <RunCodeGenDependsOn>BeforeRunCodeGen;RunCodeGenCore;AfterRunCodeGen</RunCodeGenDependsOn>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <Target Name="RunCodeGen" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
      DependsOnTargets="$(RunCodeGenDependsOn)" />

  <!-- Override BeforeRunCodeGen / AfterRunCodeGen in consuming projects
       to add pre- or post-processing around code generation. -->
  <Target Name="BeforeRunCodeGen" />
  <Target Name="RunCodeGenCore">
    <Exec Command="codegen.exe --output $(IntermediateOutputPath)generated.cs" />
  </Target>
  <Target Name="AfterRunCodeGen" />
</Project>

arguments.new_str:
<Project>
  <!--
    Do NOT disable wildcard imports — NuGet packages like MyAnalyzer.BuildTasks
    rely on ImportByWildcard to auto-import their build/ .props and .targets.
    Setting these to false silently breaks any package that ships a build hook.
  -->

  <!-- Import shared repository settings.
       The old condition checked CommonPropsImported which is defined *inside*
       Common.props — a chicken-and-egg problem. Use Exists() instead. -->
  <Import Project="shared\Common.props"
      Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)shared\Common.props')" />
</Project>
arguments.old_str:
<Project>
  <!-- Disable wildcard imports for controlled build behavior -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps>false</ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps>
    <ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>false</ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Import shared repository settings -->
  <Import Project="shared\Common.props"
      Condition="'$(CommonPropsImported)' == 'true'" />
</Project>

arguments.new_str:
<Project>
  <!--
    NOTE: CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets must be set in Directory.Build.props,
    not .targets. It was moved there. See Directory.Build.props.
  -->

  <!-- Import shared code analysis rules, guarded by Exists so fresh clones
       don't break if tools haven't been restored yet. -->
  <Import Project="$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets"
      Condition="'$(RepoToolsPath)' != '' and Exists('$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets')" />

  <!-- Renamed to avoid collision with the SDK's built-in GenerateAssemblyInfo target -->
  <Target Name="GenerateCustomAssemblyInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <Message Text="Custom assembly info generation" Importance="high" />
  </Target>

  <!-- Use the public CopyFilesToOutputDirectory target instead of the
       private _CopyFilesMarkedCopyLocal which may change between SDK versions. -->
  <Target Name="RunPostBuildChecks" AfterTargets="CopyFilesToOutputDirectory">
    <Exec Command="post-build-check.exe $(TargetPath)" />
  </Target>
</Project>

data.content:
Here's a summary of all issues found and fixed:

### Root level
| Issue | Fix |
|---|---|
| **Unconditional import** of `eng\custom-analyzers.props` breaks fresh clones | Added `Condition="Exists(...)"` guard |
| **`CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets` in `.targets`** — too late, MSBuild already loaded common targets | Moved to `Directory.Build.props` |
| **`RunCodeGen` not extensible** — other teams can't add pre/post steps | Added `RunCodeGenDependsOn` pattern with `BeforeRunCodeGen`/`AfterRunCodeGen` empty targets |

### hard/ subdirectory
| Issue | Fix |
|---|---|
| **`ImportByWildcard*=false`** silently disables NuGet package build hooks | Removed the properties |
| **Chicken-and-egg import condition** — `CommonPropsImported` is defined inside the file being guarded | Changed to `Exists()` condition |
| **`CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets` in `.targets`** | Moved to `.props` |
| **Unguarded `code-analysis.targets` import** with undefined `RepoToolsPath` | Added `Exists()` + non-empty guard |
| **`GenerateAssemblyInfo` name collision** with SDK built-in target | Renamed to `GenerateCustomAssemblyInfo` |
| **`AfterTargets="_CopyFilesMarkedCopyLocal"`** uses a private SDK target | Changed to public `CopyFilesToOutputDirectory` |
